articles.title: Math Takes Two: A test for emergent mathematical reasoning in communication
articles.titleKo: 수학은 둘이서: 의사소통 속 수학적 추론 능력 평가
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21935v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Although language models demonstrate remarkable proficiency on mathematical benchmarks, it remains unclear whether this reflects true mathematical reasoning or statistical pattern matching over learning formal syntax. Most existing evaluations rely on symbolic problems grounded in established mathematical conventions, limiting insight into the models' ability to construct abstract concepts from first principles. articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses a critical question: do language models truly perform mathematical reasoning, or are they simply matching statistical patterns based on learned syntax? Existing mathematical benchmarks rely on symbolic problems grounded in established mathematical conventions, limiting their ability to assess whether models can construct abstract concepts from first principles. To address this gap, the researchers developed "Math Takes Two," a novel benchmark designed to evaluate the emergence of mathematical reasoning through communication between two language models. This approach measures whether models can collaboratively develop new mathematical concepts through interaction, without relying on pre-existing mathematical conventions. The study has significant implications for understanding the cognitive capabilities of language models at a deeper level. However, additional research is needed to ensure diversity in inter-model communication methods and objectivity in evaluation methodologies.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 언어 모델이 수학 문제를 잘 푸는 것처럼 보이지만, 실제로 진정한 수학적 추론을 하는지 아니면 단순히 학습된 문법 패턴을 통계적으로 매칭하는 것인지를 구분하는 것이 어렵다는 문제에서 출발했습니다. 기존의 수학 평가 벤치마크들은 확립된 수학적 관례에 기반한 기호 문제들에 의존하고 있어, 모델이 기초부터 추상적 개념을 구성할 수 있는 능력을 제대로 평가하지 못하고 있습니다. 이를 해결하기 위해 연구팀은 "Math Takes Two"라는 새로운 벤치마크를 개발했으며, 이는 두 언어 모델 간의 커뮤니케이션을 통해 수학적 추론 능력의 발현을 평가하도록 설계되었습니다. 이 방법은 모델들이 기존의 수학적 관례에 의존하지 않고 상호작용을 통해 새로운 개념을 창발적으로 구성할 수 있는지를 측정합니다. 이 연구는 언어 모델의 인지 능력을 더 깊이 있게 이해하는 데 중요한 의의가 있습니다. 다만, 현재 단계에서는 모델 간 통신 방식의 다양성과 평가 방식의 객관성을 보장하기 위한 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

keyPoints: **포인트1**: 기존 수학 벤치마크의 한계 / 확립된 수학 관례에 기반해 있어 모델의 진정한 추론 능력 평가 불가 | Limitation of existing benchmarks: Reliance on established mathematical conventions prevents accurate assessment of genuine reasoning abilities

**포인트2**: 새로운 평가 방식의 제안 / 두 언어 모델 간 커뮤니케이션을 통한 수학적 추론 능력 평가 | Novel evaluation approach: Assesses mathematical reasoning through communication between two language models to observe emergent concept formation

**포인트3**: 진정한 수학적 추론 vs 패턴 매칭 구분 / 통신 기반 테스트로 통계적 패턴 매칭이 아닌 개념적 이해 여부 판단 | Distinguishing true reasoning: Communication-based testing helps differentiate between genuine mathematical understanding and statistical pattern matching
**포인트4**: 향후 연구 필요성 / 모델 간 상호작용 방식의 다양성과 평가 메커니즘의 객관성 보완 필요 | Future research needs: Requires exploration of diverse inter-model interaction methods and more objective evaluation mechanisms

articles.title: An Artifact-based Agent Framework for Adaptive and Reproducible Medical Image Processing
articles.titleKo: 의료 영상 처리를 위한 적응형 및 재현 가능한 아티팩트 기반 에이전트 프레임워크
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21936v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical imaging research is increasingly shifting from controlled benchmark evaluation toward real-world clinical deployment. In such settings, applying analytical methods extends beyond model design to require dataset-aware workflow configuration and provenance tracking. Two requirements therefore become central: \textbf{adaptability}, the ability to configure workflows according to dataset-specific conditions and evolving analytical goals; and \textbf{reproducibility}, the guarantee that all transformations and decisions are explicitly recorded
articles.aiSummary: As medical imaging research transitions from controlled laboratory benchmarks to real-world clinical deployment, new challenges emerge that extend beyond model design. Clinical deployment requires two critical capabilities: adaptability to configure analytical workflows according to dataset-specific characteristics, and reproducibility to explicitly record all transformations and analytical decisions. Researchers developed an artifact-based intelligent agent framework that achieves both adaptive workflow configuration and comprehensive provenance tracking for medical image processing. The framework systematically tracks processing steps through artifact-centered architecture, enabling automatic adjustment to different data characteristics and clinical contexts. This approach enhances the trustworthiness of AI-assisted medical image analysis and facilitates clinician acceptance of AI-generated results through transparent documentation of analytical processes. However, validation across diverse clinical settings and evaluation of real-world clinical usability remain important future directions for this research.
articles.aiSummaryKo: 의료 영상 분석 연구가 실험실의 통제된 환경에서 실제 임상 현장으로 이동하면서 새로운 과제가 대두되고 있다. 기존 의료 영상 분석 방법은 모델 개발에만 집중했지만, 실제 임상 환경에서는 각 병원의 데이터 특성에 맞게 분석 과정을 조정하고, 모든 처리 과정을 기록해야 한다는 두 가지 핵심 요구사항이 생겼다. 연구진은 인공지능 에이전트 기반 프레임워크를 개발하여 의료 영상 처리 워크플로우를 자동으로 데이터에 맞게 구성할 수 있는 '적응성'과 모든 변환 과정과 의사결정을 명확히 기록하는 '재현성'을 동시에 달성했다. 이 프레임워크는 아티팩트(결과물)를 중심으로 각 단계의 처리 과정을 체계적으로 추적한다. 이를 통해 의료 영상 분석 결과의 신뢰성을 높이고, 임상 의사들이 AI 분석 결과를 신뢰하고 수용할 수 있는 기반을 마련한다. 다만, 실제 다양한 임상 환경에서의 검증과 임상의의 실제 사용성 평가는 향후 과제로 남아있다.

This approach enhances the trustworthiness of AI-assisted medical image analysis and facilitates clinician acceptance of AI-generated results through transparent documentation of analytical processes. keyPoints: **적응성(Adaptability)** / 의료 영상 처리 워크플로우가 각 병원의 데이터 특성과 분석 목표에 따라 자동으로 조정되어 실제 임상 환경에 유연하게 대응 가능 / The framework automatically configures medical imaging workflows to match specific dataset characteristics and evolving analytical goals in clinical settings

**재현성(Reproducibility)** / 모든 영상 처리 단계와 AI의 의사결정 과정이 명확하게 기록되어 분석 결과의 신뢰성과 검증 가능성 확보 / Complete documentation of all image transformations and AI decision-making processes ensures transparency and verifiability of analytical results

**아티팩트 중심 설계** / 결과물 기반의 프로세스 추적 방식으로 각 처리 단계 간의 관계를 명확히 하고 오류 추적을 용이하게 함 / Artifact-based architecture systematically tracks relationships between processing steps and facilitates error detection and quality assurance

**임상 배포의 현실화** / 실험실 기준 평가에서 벗어나 실제 병원 환경의 데이터 다양성과 변동성을 처리할 수 있는 기반 마련 / Bridges the gap between controlled research environments and real clinical practice by addressing practical deployment challenges with heterogeneous data

articles.title: MolClaw: An Autonomous Agent with Hierarchical Skills for Drug Molecule Evaluation, Screening, and Optimization
articles.titleKo: MolClaw: 약물 분자 평가, 스크리닝 및 최적화를 위한 계층적 기술을 갖춘 자율 에이전트
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21937v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Computational drug discovery, particularly the complex workflows of drug molecule screening and optimization, requires orchestrating dozens of specialized tools in multi-step workflows, yet current AI agents struggle to maintain robust performance and consistently underperform in these high-complexity scenarios. Here we present MolClaw, an autonomous agent that leads drug molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization. It unifies over 30 specialized domain resources through a three-tier hierarchical skill architecture (70 skills in total) that
articles.aiSummary:
Drug discovery requires orchestrating dozens of specialized computational tools in complex multi-step workflows, yet current AI agents struggle with high-complexity scenarios. Researchers developed MolClaw, an autonomous AI agent designed to lead drug molecule evaluation, screening, and optimization tasks. MolClaw integrates over 30 specialized domain resources through a three-tier hierarchical skill architecture comprising 70 skills total. This multi-layered hierarchical design significantly improves AI agent performance in complex drug discovery workflows by enabling better task decomposition and resource coordination. The research demonstrates the practical applicability of AI technology in computational drug discovery. However, validation through actual clinical-stage testing is needed, and more detailed evaluation of computational costs and processing time should be conducted in future studies.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
신약 개발 과정에서 분자 구조 평가, 선별, 최적화는 수십 개의 전문 도구를 조합해야 하는 복잡한 작업입니다. 기존 AI 에이전트들은 이러한 고복잡도 작업에서 성능이 떨어지는 문제를 보였습니다. 이를 해결하기 위해 연구팀은 MolClaw라는 자율 AI 에이전트를 개발했습니다. MolClaw는 30개 이상의 전문 도메인 자원을 통합하고, 3단계 계층적 기술 구조(총 70개 기술)를 통해 약물 분자 평가, 선별, 최적화를 수행합니다. 이 다층 구조 설계는 복잡한 신약 개발 워크플로우에서 AI 에이전트의 성능을 크게 향상시킵니다. 본 연구는 계산 신약 개발 분야에서 AI 기술의 실용적 적용 가능성을 보여줍니다. 다만 실제 임상 단계까지의 검증이 필요하며, 계산 비용과 처리 시간에 대한 상세한 평가가 추가되어야 할 것으로 예상됩니다.

##

keyPoints: **계층적 기술 구조 설계** / Hierarchical Skill Architecture: 3단계 계층 구조로 70개의 전문 기술을 조직화하여 복잡한 약물 개발 작업을 효율적으로 처리 | Organizing 70 specialized skills through a 3-tier hierarchical structure enables efficient processing of complex drug development tasks

articles.title: Read the Paper, Write the Code: Agentic Reproduction of Social-Science Results
articles.titleKo: 논문을 읽고 코드를 작성하다: 사회과학 결과의 에이전트 기반 재현
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21965v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work has used LLM agents to reproduce empirical social science results with access to both the data and code. We broaden this scope by asking: Can they reproduce results given only a paper's methods description and original data? We develop an agentic reproduction system that extracts structured methods descriptions from papers, runs reimplementations under strict information isolation -- agents never see the original code, results, or paper -- and enables deterministic, cell-level comparison of reproduced outputs to the original results.
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates whether LLM-based AI agents can reproduce social science research results using only a paper's methods description and original data, without access to original code or results. Unlike previous work that provided both code and data, this research imposed strict information isolation where agents never see the original implementations or findings. The researchers developed a system that extracts structured methods descriptions from papers and enables deterministic, cell-level comparison of reproduced outputs against original results. This approach offers an automated methodology to address the reproducibility crisis in scientific research. The significance lies in demonstrating that AI can enhance research reproducibility efficiency while maintaining scientific rigor. However, limitations may exist in handling complex methodologies or exceptional circumstances not explicitly described in papers.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 대규모언어모델(LLM) 기반 AI 에이전트가 논문의 방법 설명과 원본 데이터만으로 사회과학 연구 결과를 재현할 수 있는지를 조사했습니다. 기존 연구와 달리 원본 코드나 결과를 제공하지 않고, 논문에 기술된 방법만을 사용하도록 제한했습니다. 연구팀은 논문에서 구조화된 방법 설명을 추출하고, AI 에이전트가 이를 바탕으로 코드를 작성하도록 하는 시스템을 개발했으며, 엄격한 정보 격리 조건 하에서 재현된 결과와 원본 결과를 세부 단위로 비교했습니다. 이는 과학 연구의 재현성 위기를 해결하기 위한 자동화된 방법론을 제시합니다. 연구의 의의는 AI가 과학적 엄밀성을 보장하면서 연구 재현의 효율성을 높일 수 있음을 보여주는 데 있습니다. 그러나 복잡한 방법론이나 예외적 상황에 대한 처리 능력에는 한계가 있을 수 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study investigates whether LLM-based AI agents can reproduce social science research results using only a paper's methods description and original data, without access to original code or results. Unlike previous work that provided both code and data, this research imposed strict information isolation where agents never see the original implementations or findings. The researchers developed a system that extracts structured methods descriptions from papers and enables deterministic, cell-level comparison of reproduced outputs against original results. This approach offers an automated methodology to address the reproducibility crisis in scientific research. The significance lies in demonstrating that AI can enhance research reproducibility efficiency while maintaining scientific rigor. However, limitations may exist in handling complex methodologies or exceptional circumstances not explicitly described in papers.

##
keyPoints: **방법론적 혁신** / **Methodological Innovation**: 논문의 텍스트 설명만으로부터 구조화된 방법을 추출하고 자동 코드 생성을 통해 연구 재현성을 검증하는 새로운 프레임워크 제시 / Presents a novel framework for extracting structured methods from paper text and automating code generation to verify research reproducibility.

**엄격한 정보 격리 설계** / **Strict Information Isolation**: AI 에이전트가 원본 코드, 결과, 전체 논문 내용을 보지 못하도록 제한하여 진정한 재현 능력을 평가 / Restricts AI agents from seeing original code, results, and full paper content to genuinely assess reproduction capabilities without bias.

**과학 재현성 위기 해결** / **Addressing Reproducibility Crisis**: 사회과학 연구의 재현 불가능 문제를 자동화된 AI 시스템으로 체계적으로 검증할 수 있는 가능성 제시 / Offers systematic verification of reproducibility issues in social science through automated AI systems.

**세부 단위 비교 검증** / **Cell-Level Comparison Verification**: 단순한 결과 일치가 아닌 코드 실행의 각 단계별 출력값을 비교하여 더욱 정밀한 재현성 평가 실현 / Enables more precise reproducibility assessment by comparing outputs at each computational step rather than just final results.

articles.title: The Spectral Lifecycle of Transformer Training: Transient Compression Waves, Persistent Spectral Gradients, and the Q/K--V Asymmetry
articles.titleKo: 트랜스포머 훈련의 스펙트럼 생명주기: 일시적 압축파, 지속적 스펙트럼 기울기, 그리고 Q/K-V 비대칭성

We discover three phenomena: \textbf{(1)~Transient Compression Waves:} stable rank compression propagates as a traveling wave from early to late layers, creating a dramatic gradient that peaks early then \emph{reverses} -- late layers eventually over-compress past early layers. \textbf{(2)~Persistent Spectr
articles.aiSummary:
This study presents the first systematic analysis of how weight matrix singular value spectra evolve during transformer pretraining. Researchers tracked full SVD decompositions of all weight matrices at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M-285M parameters). Three key phenomena were discovered: (1) Transient Compression Waves—stable rank compression propagates as a traveling wave from early to late layers, peaking early then reversing, with late layers eventually over-compressing beyond early layers; (2) Persistent Spectral Gradients maintained throughout training; and (3) Q/K-V asymmetry in structural organization. This research provides fundamental insights into transformer training dynamics and internal learning mechanisms. However, the study is limited to specific model scales (30M-285M parameters), which may restrict generalizability to very large-scale models and other architectures.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 트랜스포머 모델의 학습 과정에서 가중치 행렬의 특이값 스펙트럼(singular value spectra)이 어떻게 변화하는지를 처음으로 체계적으로 분석했습니다. 연구진은 30M에서 285M 파라미터 규모의 세 가지 모델에서 학습 중 25단계 간격으로 모든 가중치 행렬의 SVD 분해를 추적했습니다. 주요 발견으로는 세 가지 현상이 있습니다: (1) 초기 층에서 후기 층으로 전파되는 '압축파'가 일시적으로 나타나며, 초반에 피크를 이룬 후 역전되어 후기 층이 초기 층보다 과도하게 압축됨, (2) 학습 전반에 걸쳐 지속되는 스펙트럼 기울기 변화, (3) Q/K와 V 행렬 간의 구조적 비대칭성입니다. 이 연구는 트랜스포머의 내부 학습 역학을 이해하는 데 중요한 기초를 제공하지만, 특정 모델 규모에 한정된 실험으로 인해 초대규모 모델에서의 일반화 가능성이 제한될 수 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
This study presents the first systematic analysis of how weight matrix singular value spectra evolve during transformer pretraining. Researchers tracked full SVD decompositions of all weight matrices at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M-285M parameters). Three key phenomena were discovered: (1) Transient Compression Waves—stable rank compression propagates as a traveling wave from early to late layers, peaking early then reversing, with late layers eventually over-compressing beyond early layers; (2) Persistent Spectral Gradients maintained throughout training; and (3) Q/K-V asymmetry in structural organization. This research provides fundamental insights into transformer training dynamics and internal learning mechanisms. However, the study is limited to specific model scales (30M-285M parameters), which may restrict generalizability to very large-scale models and other architectures.

##
keyPoints: **압축파 현상** / **Transient Compression Waves**: 신경망의 초기 층에서 후기 층으로 이동하는 압축 현상이 발견되며, 처음에는 강하지만 학습이 진행되면서 역전되어 후기 층이 더 강하게 압축됨
**지속적 스펙트럼 기울기** / **Persistent Spectral Gradients**: 층 간에 일관된 스펙트럼 특성의 차이가 학습 전반에 걸쳐 유지되며 이는 신경망 구조의 본질적 특성을 반영
**Q/K-V 비대칭성** / **Q/K-V Asymmetry**: 어텐션 메커니즘에서 Query/Key 행렬과 Value 행렬이 서로 다른 구조적 특성을 가지고 있음을 발견
**모델 규모 일관성** / **Consistency Across Model Scales**: 30M부터 285M 파라미터의 다양한 규모 모델에서 동일한 현상이 반복되어 발견된 패턴의 보편성을 시사

articles.titleKo: KARL: 지식 경계 인식 강화학습을 통한 대규모 언어모델의 환각 현상 완화
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22779v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enabling large language models (LLMs) to appropriately abstain from answering questions beyond their knowledge is crucial for mitigating hallucinations. While existing reinforcement learning methods foster autonomous abstention, they often compromise answer accuracy because their static reward mechanisms, agnostic to models' knowledge boundaries, drive models toward excessive caution. In this work, we propose KARL, a novel framework that continuously aligns an LLM's abstention behavior with its evolving knowledge boundary. KARL introduces two cor
articles.aiSummary:
Large language models (LLMs) must appropriately abstain from answering questions beyond their knowledge scope to mitigate "hallucination," where models generate false information. While existing reinforcement learning methods encourage models to autonomously refuse answers, they compromise answer accuracy because static reward mechanisms push models toward excessive caution without understanding knowledge boundaries. This research proposes KARL, a novel framework that continuously aligns an LLM's abstention behavior with its evolving knowledge boundary. KARL introduces two core mechanisms that make models awareness-aware of dynamic knowledge boundaries, enabling them to answer questions they can address accurately while appropriately declining questions they cannot answer. This approach more effectively reduces hallucinations compared to existing methods while maintaining answer accuracy. However, the study was validated only within specific datasets, and further research is needed to verify generalization performance across diverse domains.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
대형언어모델(LLM)은 자신의 지식 범위를 벗어난 질문에 답변을 삼가는 능력이 중요한데, 이는 모델이 거짓 정보를 생성하는 '환각 현상'을 줄이기 위함입니다. 기존의 강화학습 방법들은 모델이 자발적으로 답변을 거절하도록 유도했지만, 정적인 보상 메커니즘이 모델을 지나치게 신중하게 만들어 정확한 답변 제공을 훼손했습니다. 본 연구에서는 KARL이라는 새로운 프레임워크를 제안하며, 이는 LLM의 추상화 행동을 진화하는 지식 경계와 지속적으로 정렬시킵니다. KARL은 모델의 동적 지식 경계를 인식하는 두 가지 핵심 메커니즘을 도입하여, 답변해야 할 질문은 정확하게 답하고 답변할 수 없는 질문에는 적절히 거절하는 균형을 맞춥니다. 이 방식은 기존 방법보다 환각 현상을 더 효과적으로 감소시키면서도 답변 정확도를 유지합니다. 다만 연구는 특정 데이터셋 범위 내에서만 검증되었으며, 실제 다양한 도메인 적용에서의 일반화 성능은 추가 연구가 필요합니다.

Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNs) provide a principled framework for modeling time-evolving interactions; however, existing TGN-based methods predominantly rely on unidirectional or single-mechanism temporal aggregation, which limits their ability to capture recursive, multi-scale temporal patterns commonly observed in real-world attack behaviors. In this paper, we propose BiTA, a Bidirectional Gated Recurre
articles.aiSummary:
This study proposes a novel AI technology for proactively detecting cyber attacks in computer networks. Existing Temporal Graph Neural Network (TGN) based methods use unidirectional and simplistic approaches to process data, failing to properly detect complex attack patterns. The research team developed BiTA (Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit-Transformer Aggregator), which combines bidirectional information flow with multi-scale temporal pattern analysis mechanisms, enabling more effective alert prediction. BiTA can capture more sophisticated recursive and multi-scale temporal patterns compared to existing methods, supporting rapid response to cyber security threats. The research has significant potential to substantially improve attack detection accuracy in real-world network environments. However, limitations include the need for performance validation in actual large-scale network environments and additional testing against diverse types of attacks.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

BiTA는 기존 방법들에 비해 더 정교한 재귀적 및 다중 규모의 시간 패턴을 포착할 수 있으며, 이를 통해 사이버 보안 위협에 대한 신속한 대응을 지원한다. 본 연구는 실제 네트워크 환경에서의 공격 탐지 정확도를 크게 향상시킬 수 있는 잠재력을 가지고 있다. 다만 실제 대규모 네트워크 환경에서의 성능 검증과 다양한 유형의 공격에 대한 추가 테스트가 필요한 한계가 있다.

articles.aiSummary:
Serving large language models efficiently requires caching Key-Value (KV) information to avoid redundant computation during text generation, but this KV cache creates a significant memory footprint that impacts operational costs. This research proposes "Stochastic KV Routing," a novel method to reduce these memory requirements. While previous studies focused on compression and eviction along the temporal axis, this work presents an orthogonal optimization approach using the depth dimension of neural networks. The team discovered that selectively sharing KV caches across different layers of the model can substantially improve memory efficiency. Unlike conventional approaches, this method minimizes performance degradation while achieving notable memory savings. However, specific quantitative performance metrics are not detailed in the abstract, limiting full assessment of the method's practical impact at this stage.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

articles.aiSummary:
This research presents a novel approach to detect doping in sports by analyzing competition performance patterns rather than relying solely on biological testing. Current anti-doping programs face significant challenges: biological testing costs over $800 per sample and has limited detection windows for many prohibited substances, making universal athlete testing impractical. To address this, researchers developed a system that analyzed 1.6 million athletic performances from over 19,000 competitions spanning 2010-2025 using eight different detection methods. The system employs visual analytics tools to identify suspicious performance patterns and anomalies in athlete results. This approach enables more efficient allocation of limited testing resources by flagging potentially suspicious cases for further investigation. However, the research has limitations, as performance improvements can result from various factors beyond doping, including enhanced training, improved nutrition, and favorable environmental conditions.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:

To address this, researchers developed a system that analyzed 1.6 million athletic performances from over 19,000 competitions spanning 2010-2025 using eight different detection methods. The system employs visual analytics tools to identify suspicious performance patterns and anomalies in athlete results. This approach enables more efficient allocation of limited testing resources by flagging potentially suspicious cases for further investigation. However, the research has limitations, as performance improvements can result from various factors beyond doping, including enhanced training, improved nutrition, and favorable environmental conditions.

articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21956v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Timely detection of concerning events is an important problem in clinical practice. In this paper, we consider the problem of conditional anomaly detection that aims to identify data instances with an unusual response, such as the omission of an important lab test. We develop a new non-parametric approach for conditional anomaly detection based on the soft harmonic solution, with which we estimate the confidence of the label to detect anomalous mislabeling. We further regularize the solution to avoid the detection of isolated examples and example
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the problem of conditional anomaly detection to timely identify concerning events in clinical practice, such as omission of important lab tests. The research team developed a novel non-parametric approach based on soft harmonic functions to estimate the confidence of labels and detect anomalous mislabeling in clinical data. The method incorporates regularization techniques to avoid false detection of isolated examples. The significance of this study lies in its potential to provide immediate clinical alerts that improve patient safety and healthcare quality in real-world medical settings. However, the limitations include the absence of validation results on actual clinical data and potential challenges in applying the method across diverse healthcare environments and systems.

##

articles.title: Multi-Task Optimization over Networks of Tasks
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.21991v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-task optimization is a powerful approach for solving a large number of tasks in parallel. However, existing algorithms face distinct limitations: Population-based methods scale poorly and remain underexplored for large task sets. Approaches that do scale beyond a thousand tasks are mostly MAP-Elites variants and rely on a fixed, discretized archive that disregards the topology of the task space. We introduce MONET (Multi-Task Optimization over Networks of Tasks), a multi-task optimization algorithm that models the task space as a graph: tas
articles.aiSummary:
This research addresses the limitations of existing multi-task optimization methods for solving thousands of tasks simultaneously. Current approaches face significant challenges: population-based methods scale poorly, while scalable variants like MAP-Elites rely on fixed, discretized archives that ignore task space topology. The researchers propose MONET (Multi-Task Optimization over Networks of Tasks), an algorithm that models the task space as a graph to capture relationships between tasks. This approach leverages correlations and structural relationships among tasks to improve optimization performance. The significance lies in demonstrating more efficient optimization is possible in complex environments with thousands of tasks. However, further validation is needed regarding graph construction methods and performance in large-scale real-world applications.

##

articles.title: Foundation models for discovering robust biomarkers of neurological disorders from dynamic functional connectivity
articles.titleKo: 동적 기능적 연결성으로부터 신경 질환의 견고한 생체표지자를 발굴하기 위한 기초 모델
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22018v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Several brain foundation models (FM) have recently been proposed to predict brain disorders by modelling dynamic functional connectivity (FC). While they demonstrate remarkable model performance and zero- or few-shot generalization, the salient features identified as potential biomarkers are yet to be thoroughly evaluated. We propose RE-CONFIRM, a framework for evaluating the robustness of potential biomarker candidates elucidated by deep learning (DL) models including FMs. From experiments on five large datasets of Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD)
articles.aiSummary:
Recent brain foundation models have been developed to predict neurological disorders by analyzing dynamic functional connectivity patterns in the brain. While these models show impressive predictive performance and work well with limited data (zero- or few-shot learning), the reliability of brain features identified as potential disease biomarkers has not been thoroughly validated. Researchers proposed RE-CONFIRM, a new evaluation framework to assess the robustness of potential biomarkers identified by deep learning and foundation models. Experiments were conducted across five large-scale brain imaging datasets, including studies on Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD). This framework helps determine whether brain activity features identified by AI are truly reliable disease indicators. The study's limitations include a focus on specific disorders, and broader application to various neurological conditions requires further investigation.

articles.titleKo: 교차 피험자 및 교차 플랫폼 변동에 강건한 이명의 휴지 상태 뇌파 바이오마커
articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22116v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Tinnitus is a prevalent auditory condition lacking objective biomarkers, motivating the search for reliable neural signatures. EEG, being a noninvasive method of brain imaging with a high temporal resolution provides a way to investigate the neural dynamics that may be associated with tinnitus. The generalizability of EEG-based tinnitus biomarkers across different datasets remains a critical challenge. Microstate theory has allowed for the characterization of quasi-stable topographic configurations in EEG, with some studies reporting altered micr
articles.aiSummary:
This study investigates neural signatures of tinnitus—a prevalent auditory condition lacking objective diagnostic biomarkers—using resting-state EEG brain imaging. Researchers employed microstate theory to characterize quasi-stable topographic patterns in EEG data and compared neural signatures between tinnitus patients and controls. The primary objective is to develop reliable biomarkers that maintain validity across different datasets and EEG recording platforms, addressing the critical generalizability challenge in clinical neuroimaging. Tinnitus currently relies on subjective patient reports for diagnosis, so objective neural biomarkers could enable precise diagnosis and treatment efficacy monitoring. The study highlights that overcoming cross-subject variability and equipment-related differences represents a significant methodological hurdle. Future large-scale, multicenter studies are necessary to validate these findings for clinical implementation.

##

articles.title: Autism genetics linked to reduced brain cell fiber density
articles.titleKo: 자폐증 유전자와 뇌 세포 섬유 밀도 감소의 연관성
articles.summary: A new large-scale genetic analysis reveals that common gene variants associated with autism are also linked to lower neurite density among the general public, suggesting a shared biological architecture between neurodevelopment and microscopic brain structure.
articles.aiSummary:
This large-scale genetic analysis reveals that common gene variants associated with autism are linked to reduced neurite density in the general population. Researchers tracked genetic variants that increase autism risk through comprehensive genomic analysis. Key findings show that individuals carrying more autism-linked genetic variants have lower dendritic fiber density, suggesting microscopic changes in brain structure. This discovery indicates that autism is rooted in fundamental biological changes during brain development, rather than being merely a behavioral disorder. The research demonstrates a shared biological mechanism between neurodevelopment and microscopic brain architecture. However, the study was conducted on the general population, so further validation is needed to determine how pronounced these changes are in individuals with clinical autism diagnosis.

##

articles.summary: arXiv:2604.22137v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Conventional scalp-based EEG systems are cumbersome to use, requiring extensive setup, restrictive wiring, and conductive gels that can dry out and limit long-term monitoring, while also carrying social stigma. As a result, there is increasing interest in in-ear EEG technology to improve comfort, convenience, and discretion for users. This work presents a personalized in-ear EEG monitor (IEEM) that simultaneously captures EEG signals from the outer ear while delivering audio playback through the same device. articles.aiSummary:
Conventional scalp-based EEG systems are cumbersome due to complex setup requirements, extensive wiring, conductive gels that dry out, and social stigma associated with their use. To overcome these limitations, researchers developed a personalized in-ear EEG monitor (IEEM) that simultaneously captures brain signals from the outer ear while delivering audio playback through the same device. The custom-molded earpiece design enables long-term brain activity monitoring without user discomfort or social inconvenience. This wearable technology has significant potential applications in medical monitoring, neuroscience research, and everyday health management. The innovation combines comfort, discretion, and functionality by integrating EEG sensing and auditory stimulation in a single device. However, this technical development paper lacks clinical validation and large-scale user testing, which represents a key limitation for real-world deployment. Future research should focus on comparative accuracy studies and longitudinal user experience assessment.

##
articles.aiSummaryKo:
기존의 두피 기반 뇌파(EEG) 측정 장비는 설치가 복잡하고 전선이 많으며 전도성 젤이 필요해 장시간 사용이 어렵고 사회적 낙인이 있다는 문제가 있습니다. 이러한 한계를 극복하기 위해 연구팀은 귀 안에 착용하는 맞춤형 뇌파 모니터링 장치(IEEM)를 개발했습니다. 이 장치는 귀 바깥쪽에서 동시에 뇌파 신호를 포집하면서 동일한 기기를 통해 음성 재생도 가능하도록 설계되었습니다. 귀에 정확히 맞도록 맞춤 제작된 이어피스를 통해 사용자는 불편함 없이 장기간 뇌 활동을 모니터링할 수 있습니다. 이 기술은 의료 모니터링, 신경과학 연구, 일상 건강 관리 등 다양한 분야에서 활용될 가능성이 높습니다. 다만 본 연구는 기술 개발 단계의 논문으로, 임상 검증과 대규모 사용자 테스트가 아직 부족하다는 한계가 있습니다.

##
articles.aiSummaryEn:
Conventional scalp-based EEG systems are cumbersome due to complex setup requirements, extensive wiring, conductive gels that dry out, and social stigma associated with their use. To overcome these limitations, researchers developed a personalized in-ear EEG monitor (IEEM) that simultaneously captures brain signals from the outer ear while delivering audio playback through the same device. The custom-molded earpiece design enables long-term brain activity monitoring without user discomfort or social inconvenience. This wearable technology has significant potential applications in medical monitoring, neuroscience research, and everyday health management. The innovation combines comfort, discretion, and functionality by integrating EEG sensing and auditory stimulation in a single device. However, this technical development paper lacks clinical validation and large-scale user testing, which represents a key limitation for real-world deployment. Future research should focus on comparative accuracy studies and longitudinal user experience assessment.

##
keyPoints: **포인트1**: 기존 두피식 EEG의 문제점 극복 / Overcomes limitations of conventional scalp-based EEG systems (complex setup, gel dependency, social stigma)
**포인트2**: 귀 안착 맞춤형 장치로 동시에 뇌파 감지와 음성 출력 가능 / Custom-molded earpiece enables simultaneous EEG signal capture and audio playback functionality
**포인트3**: 장기간 모니터링과 일상적 사용 가능성 향상 / Improved comfort and discretion enable long-term monitoring and practical daily-life applications
**포인트4**: 의료, 신경과학, 건강관리 분야의 광범위한 적용 가능성 / Potential applications span medical monitoring, neuroscience research, and personalized health management sectors

articles.aiSummary:
This study addresses the critical need for early diagnosis of repetitive subconcussive (rSC) brain injuries to enable timely clinical intervention. Conventional neuroimaging methods like fMRI fail to capture millisecond-level cortical dynamics essential for detecting early brain damage. Researchers developed a novel approach combining EEG and visual attention tasks to analyze spatiotemporal cortical features in 24 healthy participants. The method integrates dynamic hierarchical spatial features with early behavioral time-domain sensitivity, enabling detection of subtle brain activity changes at millisecond resolution. The findings suggest potential for earlier and more accurate identification of repetitive brain injuries. However, limitations include small sample size and recruitment of only healthy participants, which may not represent actual injury populations.

articles.aiSummaryKo:
이 연구는 반복적인 미세 뇌진탕(반복적 뇌손상) 초기 진단의 중요성에 주목하여 시작되었습니다. 기존의 fMRI 같은 느린 뇌영상 기법들은 밀리초 단위의 뇌 활동 변화를 포착하지 못하는 한계가 있었습니다. 연구진은 24명의 건강한 피험자를 대상으로 EEG(뇌파)와 시각 주의력 과제를 결합한 새로운 방법을 개발했습니다. 이 접근법은 뇌의 공간적 특징과 초기 시간 영역 민감도를 동적으로 분석하여 밀리초 수준의 뇌 활동 변화를 감지할 수 있습니다. 연구 결과는 반복적 뇌손상의 초기 단계를 더 빠르고 정확하게 진단할 수 있는 가능성을 제시합니다. 다만 표본 크기가 제한적이고 건강한 피험자만 참여했다는 점이 연구의 한계입니다.
